turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Responding to Feedback or a Performance Review

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I want to practice asking feedback respectfully by participating in a session with a teacher. You are a teacher. I’ll play the role of a student. Please respond as if this were a real conversation. Please respond to me about my follow-up questions about performance and goals and give me advice.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, happy to help. What would you like feedback on today?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Hi, we've just finished our group project. I'd appreciate your feedback on my contribution and communication.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course! I’d be happy to give you some feedback. Overall, your contribution was really solid, and your communication was clear and effective. Is there any specific area you’d like me to focus on or any particular aspect you’re curious about?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “I want to know about my voice, too big or too small.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely! So, your voice was generally clear and confident, but there were moments where it could be a bit more concise. I’d suggest focusing on pacing and clarity. Do you have any particular parts of your presentation you’d like to refine further?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “I understand. I'll try to speak more better.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That sounds great! And I’m sure with a bit of practice, you’ll get even more comfortable. Is there any specific part of the project or any skill you’d like to work on next?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Have you seen improvement in any particular area?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, definitely! I've noticed that your organization of ideas has improved quite a bit, and you seem more confident in presenting your points. Keep building on that, and I’m sure you’ll keep making great progress! Is there any area you’d like more targeted feedback on?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Could you give an example of something I did this well and something I could improve?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely! For example, you did a great job of summarizing key points clearly during the presentation, which really helped the group. One area for improvement could be to slow down just a bit when you’re explaining complex ideas, so that everyone can follow along easily. Does that make sense?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Have you seen no improvement in any particular area?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, definitely! I’ve noticed that your engagement during group discussions has gotten a lot stronger, and your questions have been more insightful. One area to keep working on is maybe maintaining a consistent pace, especially when you’re nervous. But overall, you’re doing really well! Anything else you’d like to know?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “What do you mean?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What I mean is that sometimes you might speed up a bit when you’re feeling nervous, and that can make it harder for others to follow along. So just focusing on a steady pace can help. But you’re definitely making great progress! Let me know if there’s anything else you’d like to dive into.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So do you mean I should change my speaking speed?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Exactly! Just a slight adjustment to keep your pace steady and calm can really help your audience follow you more easily. It’s all about finding that balance. And I’m sure you’ll do great with a bit of practice!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “OK. I understand.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Awesome! If you have any more questions or need more feedback along the way, just let me know. I’m here to help!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “What would you recommend I focus on improving next?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I’d recommend focusing on practicing your clarity and confidence, especially in how you structure your points. You could also try a bit of practice with pauses and breathing to help maintain that steady pace. And if you’d like, we can definitely do some targeted practice sessions as well!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Do you think a short training or workshop would help with that?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely, I think a short training or workshop would be really beneficial! It can give you focused practice, direct feedback, and some hands-on techniques to improve even faster. Let me know if you’d like any recommendations or if you want to set something up!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “I want to know your recommendation.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! I’d recommend looking into some local or online workshops that focus on public speaking and communication skills. There are also plenty of great resources on platforms like Coursera or Udemy. And if you want, I can definitely help you find some specific ones tailored to your goals!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Could we schedule a follow-up meeting in a few weeks?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That sounds like a great idea! I’d be more than happy to follow up and see how things are going. Just let me know the best time and date, and we can definitely schedule that check-in. Looking forward to it!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Next Friday is good.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect! Next Friday works for me. I’ll make sure to set a reminder, and we can catch up on your progress then. If you need anything before that, just let me know!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “OK, thank you very much. I'll make those changes and send the update by Friday.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That sounds wonderful! I’m looking forward to hearing about your progress. If you have any questions in the meantime, just reach out. Good luck, and I’ll talk to you soon!
